excerpt.zh:
路遥幽径深，落月送游人。
垂眉思孙山，寄魂与枯坟。
露润裤管处，虫草哀秋暮。
烈风摧残骨，雁去留人骷。
旷野曾绿野，屋舍绝人烟。
孤杆不住斜，细流腐落叶。
望愁垄心头，启明坠意楼。
涸田待春朝，少年正拂晓。
仰首即触天，顿足震余年。
郁结初心果，造福人世间。
excerpt.en:
Long road, deep winding path, the setting moon sees off a traveler.
Head bowed, thoughts of exam hopes, soul left at a barren grave.
Dew wets the pant legs, insects mourn the autumn dusk.
Fierce wind rends frail bones, wild geese leave a human skull behind.
Where once were green fields, now homes have no chimney smoke.
A lone pole leans crooked, a thin stream rots fallen leaves.
Grief knots at the chest, the morning star sinks from the tower.
Parched fields wait for spring dawn — a boy stands at daybreak.
Lift his head, he nearly touches the sky; stamp his feet, he shakes the years ahead.
The first fruit of his heart, to bring good to the world of men.

excerpt.zh: 进入大学，就像打开一扇通往只存在于书本中的世界的大门……
excerpt.en: Starting university is like opening a door to a world you have only read about...
